description: You are analyzing oral dosage data for Theophylline using the built-in 'Theoph' dataset. Perform a Bayesian population pharmacokinetic analysis focusing on the elimination phase (Time > 1 hour) by modeling 'log(conc)' as linear with Time. Fit two hierarchical models using 'brms' or 'rstanarm' with default or weakly informative priors (4 chains, 2000+ iterations): Model A with random intercepts only (1 | Subject) and Model B with random intercepts and slopes (Time | Subject). Compare the models using LOO-CV. Save the analysis script to '/home/ga/RProjects/output/pk_bayesian_analysis.R' and export the following results to the same directory: 'pk_population_params.csv' (summary of fixed/random effects for the best model with columns: parameter, estimate, lower95, upper95, rhat, ess_bulk), 'pk_convergence.csv' (Rhat and ESS diagnostics for all parameters with columns: parameter, rhat, ess_bulk), 'pk_model_comparison.csv' (LOO comparison results with columns: model, elpd_loo, looic), 'pk_posterior_predictive.png' (posterior predictive check density overlay), and 'pk_individual_fits.png' (observed data vs predicted regression lines/intervals for each subject).
